world.title: 'Picture abhi baaki hai': Iran's Bollywood jab at Trump amid Hormuz blockade; warns missile boats 'warming up'
world.summary: Iran responded to U.S. President Donald Trump's assertion that its navy was "finished" by displaying its fast missile boats. The Iranian consulate in Mumbai shared a video referencing a famous dialogue from a Shah Rukh Khan film, emphasizing that the situation is not over. This comes amid ongoing tensions related to the Hormuz Strait blockade.

world.title: Iran Consulate in Mumbai responds to Trump's threats with Shah Rukh Khan's dialogue
world.summary: The Iranian Consulate in Mumbai responded to U.S. President Donald Trump's assertion that Iran's Navy was "finished" by quoting actor Shah Rukh Khan's dialogue, "Abhi toh sirf trailer hai, picture abhi baaki hai," implying that the situation is far from over. The statement highlighted Iran's readiness, referencing its fast missile boats as a sign of ongoing military capability.

world.title: What is House File 2513 that seeks to change H-1B Visas and has already passed Iowa House and cleared initial stages in Senate
world.summary: Iowa is considering House File 2513, a law that aims to alter the hiring process for foreign workers on H-1B visas at universities. The bill has successfully passed the Iowa House and has progressed through initial stages in the Senate. If enacted, it could significantly impact how educational institutions in Iowa engage with international talent.

business.title: Gold bulls retain intraday control as Iran hopes weigh on USD
business.summary: Gold (XAU/USD) reached a new daily high during the early European session on Tuesday but did not maintain momentum, staying below the $4,800 level. The market sentiment is influenced by recent US-Iran peace talks that did not succeed, yet investors remain optimistic about potential developments. Overall, gold bulls maintain intraday control amid fluctuating market conditions.

business.title: As Anthropic’s new AI model Mythos sparks fears in Canada, AI minister says: We are taking ...
business.summary: Anthropic’s new AI model, Claude Mythos, has raised concerns in Canada regarding potential cybersecurity risks. Regulators, banks, and government officials have issued warnings about the unprecedented threats posed by the technology. In response, the federal government has stated that it is taking the situation seriously and is actively addressing the implications of this powerful AI model.

business.title: Renault Duster Techno+ Mid Variant Reach Showrooms - Walkaround Video
business.summary: Renault has begun delivering the Duster Techno+ mid variant to showrooms, featuring enhanced equipment compared to the Techno trim. The new Duster, anticipated for 2026, has garnered positive feedback from SUV buyers and impressed media during a recent drive in Dehradun, Uttarakhand. This launch marks a significant step in Renault's SUV offerings, catering to consumer demand.

technology.title: Apple Rolls Out iOS 26.5 Developer Beta 2 with Maps Enhancements and RCS Encryption Testing
technology.summary: Apple has launched iOS 26.5 developer beta 2, enhancing Maps and testing encrypted RCS messaging. This update focuses on refinements rather than new features, building on previous beta changes. Additionally, it introduces new subscription flexibility for developers. The beta is available for supported iPhones, allowing developers to explore these improvements and provide feedback ahead of the official release.

technology.title: Motorola Edge 70 Pro Spotted on TDRA Certification Website; Global Launch Seems Imminent
technology.summary: The Motorola Edge 70 Pro has been spotted on the TDRA certification website, indicating a potential imminent launch in India. The smartphone, identified by model number XT2607-1, is expected to feature a Snapdragon 8 series chipset and a 50-megapixel selfie camera. Flipkart has hinted at its arrival, although an official launch date has not yet been announced.

technology.title: Indian-origin CEO of USD 400M company blocks startup from conference over conflict
technology.summary: Rocketlane CEO Srikrishnan Ganesan blocked Lab0 founder Lakshya Gupta from attending a professional services conference due to a perceived conflict of interest, as both companies operate in similar sectors. This decision has sparked controversy within the startup community, particularly since Lab0 is backed by Y Combinator. Gupta expressed disappointment over the exclusion from the event.

sports.title: 'Rs 8.1 crore in a day': Bengaluru fitness event's high entrance fee sparks debate, internet divided
sports.summary: A viral post revealing Rs 8.1 crore earnings from a Bengaluru fitness event has ignited debate over the increasing costs of marathons in urban India. As participation fees rise, discussions focus on the economics of these events and their accessibility. The situation reflects broader concerns about the affordability of fitness activities in major cities like Bengaluru.

sports.title: Ishan Kishan isn’t MS Dhoni, ready to give up wicketkeeping for SRH captaincy: ‘There was some miscommunication’
sports.summary: Ishan Kishan discussed the difficulties of leading while wicketkeeping, noting it can hinder communication with bowlers. Unlike MS Dhoni, who succeeded in both roles, Kishan expressed willingness to relinquish wicketkeeping for the captaincy of Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH). He acknowledged some miscommunication during matches and emphasized the need for clarity in leadership to enhance team performance.

sports.title: ‘Riyan Parag just there for style and does nothing else’: Srikkanth goes hammer and tongs at RR skipper
sports.summary: Ex-India captain Srikkanth criticized Rajasthan Royals skipper Riyan Parag, stating he is "there only for style" and questioning his batting performance. During a match against SRH, Parag's choice to not utilize veteran all-rounder Ravindra Jadeja as a bowler surprised many, as he opted to bowl an over himself instead, raising further concerns about his decision-making.

health.title: 10-minute walk after meals can help control blood sugar spikes: Why timing matters more than step count and how to make it a daily habit
health.summary: A 10-minute walk after meals can effectively help control blood sugar spikes, according to recent research. While many focus on achieving daily step counts, the timing of these steps is crucial. Walking shortly after eating allows the body to respond differently, making this simple habit beneficial for blood sugar management. Incorporating this practice into daily routines can enhance health outcomes.

health.title: Diabetes, hypertension, and the rising health inequities in Indian cities
health.summary: In Indian cities, rising non-communicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension highlight significant health inequities, particularly in urban informal settlements. Individuals, such as 60-year-old Sakina from Mumbai, face challenges in accessing affordable, quality healthcare. The situation underscores the urgent need for community-level interventions to promote health equity and improve care accessibility for vulnerable populations in these areas.

health.title: India's cancer cases hit 15.7 lakh; delayed diagnosis main challenge
health.summary: India's cancer cases are expected to reach approximately 15.7 lakh by 2025. Doctors highlight that 60 to 70 percent of patients are diagnosed at advanced stages, which hampers survival improvements despite advancements in treatment. The challenge of delayed diagnosis remains significant, impacting the effectiveness of cutting-edge therapies like CAR-T cell treatment in improving patient outcomes.

science.title: NASA Astronaut Christina Koch’s Stirring Speech Over Artemis II Mission To The Moon Goes Viral [WATCH]
science.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, which launched on April 10th from Launch Complex 39B, aims to explore the far side of the Moon. Astronaut Christina Koch delivered a notable speech emphasizing the mission's significance and unity. The mission combines scientific research with human exploration, marking a step forward in lunar exploration and preparing for future missions beyond Earth.

science.title: Astronomers spot powerful ‘space laser’ from colliding galaxies 8 billion light
science.summary: Astronomers have identified the most distant and powerful 'space laser,' known as a megamaser, originating from colliding galaxies located 8 billion light-years away. Utilizing the MeerKAT telescope and gravitational lensing techniques, scientists gained insights into the merging galaxies and conditions of the early universe. This discovery enhances understanding of cosmic phenomena and the evolution of galaxies over time.

science.title: How did Artemis II crew work out in space in zero gravity? Video of astronaut Victor Glover will blow your mind
science.summary: NASA astronaut Victor Glover showcased a flywheel device for exercising in zero gravity aboard the Orion spacecraft during the Artemis II mission. This device enables both aerobic and resistance workouts. The Artemis II spacecraft successfully completed a 10-day journey around the Moon, marking the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years, with a crew of four astronauts.

entertainment.title: 'Too Much Fabrication, Too Many Lies': Vijay Varma On Trolls, 'Gaalis' After Breakup With Tamannaah | Exclusive
entertainment.summary: Vijay Varma discusses the scrutiny and negativity he has faced over the past year, particularly following his breakup with Tamannaah Bhatia. He expresses concern about a culture where those who criticize receive support, while victims endure backlash. Varma highlights the challenges of dealing with online trolls and misinformation, emphasizing the impact of this environment on individuals.

entertainment.title: Pakistan media watchdog issues notice to channel for running 'Indian content' while reporting news of Asha Bhosle's death
entertainment.summary: Pakistan's media watchdog has issued a show-cause notice to a prominent news channel for broadcasting "Indian content" while reporting on the death of renowned singer Asha Bhosle. The notice highlights a violation of the existing ban on Indian content in Pakistan, which has been enforced since 2018. The channel faces scrutiny for its adherence to these regulations.

entertainment.title: Late Asha Bhosle's granddaughter Zanai Bhosle expresses gratitude for honoring her grandmother: 'She's seen it all from above'
entertainment.summary: Zanai Bhosle expressed gratitude for the honors bestowed upon her late grandmother, legendary singer Asha Bhosle, who passed away at 92 on April 12, 2026. Asha was laid to rest with full state honors at Mumbai's Shivaji Park. In an emotional Instagram post, Zanai thanked everyone for their love and respect, emphasizing her grandmother's deserving legacy.
